title: Calcitrapa rupestris
taxonomy: Calcitrapa rupestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is commonly known as a type of rock cress. Calcitrapa rupestris is a member of the Brassica genus and is closely related to other plants such as cabbage and mustard. The classification of Calcitrapa rupestris places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Brassicales. This classification indicates its position within the broader plant kingdom and its relationships with other plants.

properties: Calcitrapa rupestris, also known as Rock Samphire or Sea Spurvalwort, has been traditionally used for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and gout. Its leaves and stems have been used in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant's high levels of vitamin C and other nutrients may also contribute to its potential health benefits. intro: Calcitrapa rupestris, also known as Rock Calamint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues due to its soothing and calming effects. Bioactive constituents of Calcitrapa rupestris include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Calcitrapa rupestris is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed to alleviate stress, promote relaxation, and support overall well-being.
